Journal.Title: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition

Article.ArticleTitle: The data do not seem to support a benefit to BCAA supplementation during periods of caloric restriction.

Abstract.AbstractText: J Int Soc Sports Nutr 13:1-015-0112-9, 2016 describe the efficacy of branched chain amino acid (BCAA) supplementation and resistance training for maintaining lean body mass during a calorie-restricted diet, and claim that this occurs with concurrent losses in fat mass. However, the reported results appear to be at odds with the data presented on changes in fat mass. This letter discusses the issues with the paper.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Providence Medical Research Center, Providence Sacred Heart Medical Center and Children's Hospital, Research Discovery Lab, Spokane, WA USA.
Department of Health Sciences, Lehman College, Bronx, NY USA.

Reference.Citation: Dudgeon WD, Kelley EP, Scheett TP. In a single-blind, matched group design: branched-chain amino acid supplementation and resistance training maintains lean body mass during a caloric restricted diet. J Int Soc Sports Nutr. 2016;13:1-015–0112-9. doi: 10.1186/s12970-015-0112-9.
